dto.creator: achriston

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
• Set up the standard neonatal test environment as shown in Chapter 2.1
  • Ensure, all devices are running and connected to the Network
  • Ensure, that the TAI-Server application is running on the notebook connected to the ASL-5000
  • Start the DUT
  • If not already done run all calibrations and tests in the “Preop check”-tab with the correct humidification type
  • Set the leakage valve to 20% (±5%) by using Vti and Vte of the IMT Flow Analyser (Vte = 0.8 * Vti). You can run “Vent Mode: Set Leakage for Neonatal” on https://medical-portal.hamilton.ch/apps/ait-mgmt/testCases/66/revisions/0 to set the leakage valve.
  • Run the last released version of the test case “[STBR] PPL.0002 Ventilation Mode (DuoPAP) neonatal automatic FULL” with the ID 104 on Medical Portal: AIT Management.
  • After the test is executed, export the test results and copy them into the Tables of the test-case.
testSectionDTOList.title: Test Preparation

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the applied high pressure (Phigh) and PEEP are tested. The sequence for this test is:

(ASL) -> (Setup) -> (Exec) -> (Evaluation) -> (CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements.

(Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.

(Exec)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation
  • Waits for 20 seconds

(Evaluation)-Block:

  • Asserts PEEP measured by the ventilator
  • Asserts peak pressure measured by the ventilator
  • Asserts PEEP measured by the flow analyser
  • Asserts peak pressure measured by the flow analyser

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation
testSectionDTOList.title: Phigh and PEEP

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the effects of the VT-High setting are tested. The test is passed, if

  • an application of 111% of the set VT-High limit causes a "VT-High" alarm, but the ventilation is continued as if no alarm was present.
  • an application of 200% of the set VT-High limit causes an "Vt high: breath terminated" alarm
  • the applied tidal volume on an "Vt high: breath terminated" alarm is 1.5 times the set "VT-High" limit and the inspiratory time is shorter than before the alarm. (The applied breath gets aborted)

The test uses three control-variables to evaluate the criterions above:

  • [is_valid]: is initially set to 1. If a script finds a value outside of the tolerances, the variable is set to -1
  • [error_code]: is initially set to 200. If [is_valid] = -1, then the value in [error_code] represents the script that reported a failed value
  • [successful_scripts]: counts the number of executed scripts.

The sequence for this test is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec |1/3)->(Exec |2/3)->(Exec |3/3)->(Scripting)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements.

(Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.

(Exec |1/3)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits for 20 seconds.
  • Stores one measurement of the inspiration time, expiration time, PEEP and inspiration volume measured by the flow analyser in the variables [INSPT_FLOWANALYSER_START], [EXPT_FLOWANALYSER_START], [PEEP_FLOWANALYSER_START], [VTI_FLOWANALYSER_START]

(Exec |2/3)-Block:

  • Sets the VT-High alarm to 45ml (approximately 90% of the currently applied expiration volume)
  • Waits for the "VT-High" alarm. Note: the internal code for "VT-High" alarm is 141002
  • Waits for 15 seconds
  • Stores one measurement of the inspiration time, expiration time, PEEP and inspiration volume measured by the flow analyser in the variables [INSPT_FLOWANALYSER_MIDDLE], [EXPT_FLOWANALYSER_MIDDLE], [PEEP_FLOWANALYSER_MIDDLE], [VTI_FLOWANALYSER_MIDDLE].

(Exec |3/3)-Block:

  • Sets the VT-High alarm to 25ml (approximately 50% of the applied expiration volume at the start of the test)
  • Waits for the "Vt high: breath terminated" alarm. Note: the internal code for "Vt high: breath terminated" alarm is 133002
  • Waits for 15 seconds
  • Stores one measurement of the inspiration time, expiration time, PEEP and inspiration volume measured by the flow analyser in the variables [INSPT_FLOWANALYSER_END], [EXPT_FLOWANALYSER_END], [PEEP_FLOWANALYSER_END], [VTI_FLOWANALYSER_END].

(Scripting)-Block:

  • Sets control variables [is_valid] = 1, [error_code] = 200 and [successful_scripts] = 0
  • Evaluates, if the stored variables in (Exec |1/3) are the same as in (Exec |2/3)
  • Evaluates, if the stored inspiration time in (Exec |3/3) is smaller than in (Exec |1/3)

(Evaluation)-Block:

  • Evaluates, if all scripts in (Scripting) were executed
  • Evaluates, that [is_valid] is still 1
  • Evaluates, that [error_code] is 200
  • Evaluates the applied inspiration volume stored in [VTI_FLOWANALYSER_END]

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.
testSectionDTOList.title: ........Check Alarm: High Volume

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman: Capturing the breath values after adjusting VT-Alarm-limit to ~50%
testSectionDTOList.title: ................(Exec | 3/3)Check alarms: High Volume

testElementDTOList.targetRawValue: Check, that Inspiration time of the first two captures is the same
Check, that expiration time of the first two captures is the same
Check, that PEEP of the first two captures is the same
Check, that Inspiration volume of the first two captures is the same
Check, that Inspiration gets aborted (lower Inspiration time than before)
Check, that all scripts were successfully executed
Check, that no script found a invalid result

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the effects of the VT-High setting are tested. The test is passed, if

  • an application of 111% of the set VT-High limit causes a "VT-High" alarm, but the ventilation is continued as if no alarm was present.
  • an application of 200% of the set VT-High limit causes an "Vt high: breath terminated" alarm
  • the applied tidal volume on an "Vt high: breath terminated" alarm is 1.5 times the set "VT-High" limit and the inspiratory time is shorter than before the alarm. (The applied breath gets aborted)

The test uses three control-variables to evaluate the criterions above:

  • [is_valid]: is initially set to 1. If a script finds a value outside of the tolerances, the variable is set to -1
  • [error_code]: is initially set to 200. If [is_valid] = -1, then the value in [error_code] represents the script that reported a failed value
  • [successful_scripts]: counts the number of executed scripts.

The sequence for this test is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec |1/3)->(Exec |2/3)->(Exec |3/3)->(Scripting)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements.

(Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.

(Exec |1/3)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits for 20 seconds.
  • Stores one measurement of the inspiration time, expiration time, PEEP and inspiration volume measured by the flow analyser in the variables [INSPT_FLOWANALYSER_START], [EXPT_FLOWANALYSER_START], [PEEP_FLOWANALYSER_START], [VTI_FLOWANALYSER_START]

(Exec |2/3)-Block:

  • Sets the VT-High alarm to 85ml (approximately 90% of the currently applied expiration volume)
  • Waits for the "VT-High" alarm. Note: the internal code for "VT-High" alarm is 141002
  • Waits for 15 seconds
  • Stores one measurement of the inspiration time, expiration time, PEEP and inspiration volume measured by the flow analyser in the variables [INSPT_FLOWANALYSER_MIDDLE], [EXPT_FLOWANALYSER_MIDDLE], [PEEP_FLOWANALYSER_MIDDLE], [VTI_FLOWANALYSER_MIDDLE].

(Exec |3/3)-Block:

  • Waits for the "Vt high: breath terminated" alarm. Note: the internal code for "Vt high: breath terminated" alarm is 133002
  • Waits for 15 seconds
  • Stores one measurement of the inspiration time, expiration time, PEEP and inspiration volume measured by the flow analyser in the variables [INSPT_FLOWANALYSER_END], [EXPT_FLOWANALYSER_END], [PEEP_FLOWANALYSER_END], [VTI_FLOWANALYSER_END].

(Scripting)-Block:

  • Sets control variables [is_valid] = 1, [error_code] = 200 and [successful_scripts] = 0
  • Evaluates, if the stored variables in (Exec |1/3) are the same as in (Exec |2/3)
  • Evaluates, if the stored inspiration time in (Exec |3/3) is smaller than in (Exec |1/3)

(Evaluation)-Block:

  • Evaluates, if all scripts in (Scripting) were executed
  • Evaluates, that [is_valid] is still 1
  • Evaluates, that [error_code] is 200
  • Evaluates the applied inspiration volume stored in [VTI_FLOWANALYSER_END]

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the signalization of the alarm “Apnea” and the activation of the backup ventilation with the alarm “Apnea ventilation” are tested. The test is passed, if:

  • The alarm “Apnea” is present during the 1st breath after start of ventilation with the setting “Apnea Backup” disabled.
  • The alarm “Apnea ventilation” is activated within 15 seconds, but not before 13 seconds since the detection of the start of the 2nd breath with the setting “Apnea Backup” enabled. The Backup-mode PCV+ must then be active.
  • Within 30 seconds after the ASL switches to active breathing, DuoPAP is the active mode on the ventilator.

The sequence for this test is:

(ASL |1/2) -> 1st (Setup |1/2) -> (Exec |1/2) -> 2nd (Setup |1/2) -> (Evaluation |1/2) -> (ASL |2/2) -> (Evaluation |2/2) -> (CleanUp)

(ASL |1/2)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements. At this stage, the ASL is passive.

1st (Setup |1/2)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements. Apnea Backup is set to off.

(Exec |1/2)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits 60 seconds. Including the blower initialization time, the first Low-Phase is still present.
  • Assert that the applied pressure is equal to PEEP of 5mbar. Assert that alarm “Apnea” (alarm ID = 141010) is active.
  • Wait until the applied pressure exceeds (PEEP + 1mbar). This indicates the start of the second breath.

2nd (Setup |1/2)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements. Apnea Backup is set to on.

(Evaluation |1/2)-Block:

  • Asserts, that the alarm “Apnea Ventilation” (alarm ID = 141012) is activated within 15 seconds, but not before 13 seconds, since the detection of the start of the 2nd breath.
  • Asserts, that Backup-Mode PCV+ is active.

(ASL |2/2)-Block: Activates active breathing on the ASL.

(Evaluation |2/2)-Block: Asserts, that the ventilator switches back to DuoPAP within 30 seconds after the activation of the ASL.

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation
testSectionDTOList.title: ........Check Apnea Backup

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
Set apnea backup to OFF for the first breath. 

(Reason: The blower initialization delay between start of ventilation and first breath makes it impossible in the first breath to define an expected time value when DuoPAP switches to backup-mode. The verificiation will be done in the second breath.)
testSectionDTOList.title: ................(Setup |1/2)Apnea Backup

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
General information to explain this (Exec)-block:

1. Start ventilation.

2. Wait 60s (due to the Blower Initialization time delay of (2-3seconds), the first mandatory breath (Rate = 1 b/min) with Plow-Phase is active (i.e. applied pressure is equal to PEEP) ).

3. Assert that applied pressure is equal to PEEP of 5mbar to verify the active Plow-Phase. Assert that Apnea Alarm (141010) is active.

4. Wait until applied pressure is greater than 6mbar (i.e. second breath with Phigh-Phase starts).  This indicates that the second mandatory breath with Phigh-phase has started. This should be done within < 5 seconds.
testSectionDTOList.title: ................(Exec |1/2)Apnea Backup

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman: Set apnea backup to ON during the second breath.
testSectionDTOList.title: ................(Setup |1/2)Apnea Backup

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
15 seconds after the start of the second mandatory breath,  Apnea ventilation alarm is active. This indicates that Duopap has switched to backup mode.

1. approximately 13 seconds after AIT detects the start of the second mandatory breath, none alarm is allowed to be active.

2. Within 2 seconds apnea ventilation alarm is active (alarm id = 141012). 

3. Assert that PCV+ (backup mode) is active.
testSectionDTOList.title: ................(Evaluation |1/2)Apnea Backup

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the minimal applied expiration time is tested. For each partial test, the sequence is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec)->(Scripting)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements.

(Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.

(Exec)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits for a given time interval for the ventilator tor each steady state.
  • Stores one measurement of the expiration time measured by the Flow-Analyser.

(Scripting)-Block: Converts the measurement from the Flow-Analyzer from “s” to “ms”. Note: The value is multiplied due to insufficient capabilities of AIT to evaluate floating-point numbers.

(Evaluation)-Block: Compares the measurement to the expected value.

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.
testSectionDTOList.title: TexpMin

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
All patient breaths are detected. This shows that the expiration phase of a spontaneous patient-triggered breath can be terminated after 0.2 s. 

Here, the defined ASL-settings lead the applied inspiration phase time.
testSectionDTOList.title: ........TexpMin with patient effort Texp=0.2s

testElementDTOList.targetRawValue: If first measurement is taken, while trigger was not detected, take the second measurement
Factor Expiration time by a factor of 1000

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman: The assertion on tExpPat does not work, since this value is set to Invalid in DuoPAP
testSectionDTOList.title: ................(Evaluation)TexpMin with patient effort Texp=0.2s

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
Only each 2nd patient effort is detected. This shows that patient efforts inside the TexpMin-window are ineffective. The expected applied expiration phase time is 1.1s.

Here, the defined ASL-settings lead the applied inspiration phase time.
testSectionDTOList.title: ........TexpMin with ineffective patient effort Texp=1.1s

testElementDTOList.targetRawValue: Factor Expiration time by a factor of 1000

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman: The assertion on tExpPat does not work, since this value is set to Invalid in DuoPAP
testSectionDTOList.title: ................(Evaluation)TexpMin with ineffective patient effort Texp=1.1s

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the applied time of the minimal-inspiration state of a ETS-controlled spontaneous breath is tested with Thigh = 0.3s and Thigh = 40s. TinspMin is equal to 0.1s.

The first Thigh-setting (0.3s) evaluates the duration of the TinspMin-State in the Tlow-Phase. The 2nd Thigh-setting (40s) evaluates the duration of the TinspMin-State in the Thigh-Phase.

The sequence is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec)->(Scripting)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets the ASL to have appropriate patient breathing. The spontaneous breath should be as short as possible, such that it switches to expiration phase exactly after TinspMin. 

(Setup)-Block:

  • Sets the ventilator settings including ETS = 80 % and Thigh = 0.3s or Thigh = 40s.
  • Sets the ventilator rate to 1 b/min to avoid that  time-controlled mandatory breaths influence the test.

(Exec)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits 15s.
  • Records the measured inspiration time of two breaths read the Flow Analyser.

(Scripting)-Block:

  • Takes the measurement with the lower value.
  • Compares the measurement to the expected value of TinspMin + 10ms
  • Store the result of the comparison

(Evaluation)-Block: Evaluates the result

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.
testSectionDTOList.title: TinspMin
........TinspMin with patient effort ETS = 80% and Thigh = 0.3s
................(ASL)TinspMin with patient effort ETS = 80% and Thigh = 0.3s
................(Setup)TinspMin with patient effort ETS = 80% and Thigh = 0.3s
................(Exec)TinspMin with patient effort ETS = 80% and Thigh = 0.3s

testElementDTOList.targetRawValue: FA_TI= FA_TI_2 if FA_TI > FA_TI_2 else FA_TI
Convert to ms, define expected TinspMin and comparison with storage of the result.

Convert to ms, define expected TinspMin and comparison with storage of the result.
